label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, February's last day is usually the 28th, unless it's a leap year when it's the 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year, right? Because leap years are divisible by 4. 2000 was a leap year, then 2004. So 2001 isn't, so her birthday would be February 28, 2001. Now, today is her 16th birthday. So that would mean today's date is February 28, 2017. Because 2001 + 16 = 2017. So today is 02/28/2017. The question is asking for the date 10 days ago. So subtract 10 days from February 28, 2017. But wait, let's check if 2017 is a leap year. 2017 divided by 4 is 504.25, so not a leap year. Therefore, February has 28 days. So if today is the 28th, then 10 days ago would be... Let's count backwards. Starting from the 28th, subtract 10 days. So 28 minus 10 is 18. So that would be February 18, 2017. So the answer would be 02/18/2017, which is option B. But wait, wait. Let me double-check. If today is the 28th, then: Day 0: 02/28/2017 (today) 1 day ago: 02/27/2017 2 days ago: 02/26 ... 10 days ago: 02/18. Yeah, because 28 minus 10 is 18. So that's still in February. So the date is 02/18/2017, which is option B. So the answer should be B. Let me check the options again. The options are: A) 02/28/2017 (today, not 10 days ago) B) 02/18/2017 (this seems correct) C) 03/18/2017 (March would be if we went over feb) D) 02/13/2017 (15 days ago?) E) 02/25/2017 (3 days ago) F) 03/04/2017 (March again, but not needed) So B is the correct one. But wait, let me make sure there's no trick here. Like maybe her birthday is on Feb 29 in a leap year? Wait, but 2001 is not a leap year. So she was born on Feb 28. Then in 2017, which is not a leap year,
